mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term predictive IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistical significance during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with artificially low policy rates compresses real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Markets price in inflation pass-through to earnings, yielding positive returns in more than 70% of triggered cases, or the real rate differential shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and consumer spending base, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from earnings growth, resulting in negative forward returns in more than 55% of expansion periods, or the signal loses predictive power during automation-driven employment shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Exceptionally rapid real GDP growth signals economic overheating and peak demand, prompting investors to anticipate central bank tightening and recession risks, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High GDP growth consistently correlates with earnings beats and positive market returns, or the signal fails to predict downturns in more than 60% of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial output and decreases in unemployment confirm synchronized supply and labor market strength, reducing recession probabilities and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of synchronized expansion periods due to valuation compression.
